Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

I was in the program management office and worked in Rent and Management Solutions that dealt with a software system called Voucher and its modules. My family is from the Upper West Side of Manhattan, New York. My dad was a park worker who only finished 10th grade but was a social reformer and helped many kids stay out of trouble. My mother worked for Bloomingdales and then did volunteer work.

In 2000 my husband and I moved from New York to Key West and we have lived here ever since. I am one of the original Mayor’s Fest in the Spring visitors to Key West. It is great to have a long weekend without the drive and I get to see my friends. We have festivals all year round so there is never a reason not to visit here and it’s always great fun. I love to visit the Keys and Key West is the “Last” one with the most style and personality. We are filled with creative minds and I am filled with energy with much to do in Key West. I look forward to every morning and I am looking forward to achieving my goals and seeing all of you in the Fall and creating new ones in 2019. Lastly, my mother passed away in April of this year. She was 94 years old. She loved the FFF so much.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.

Answer: I am 6 foot tall, 200 pounds, 59 years old, born and raised in the city of Los Angeles. I have no college degree but I am well educated on the streets and in the job markets. I am a Christian who has had several progressive careers. I did not graduate high school because I dropped out of school at age 17 because my girlfriend became pregnant. I left her father to the fate of rearing a child with his junkie girlfriend so I could enter the Army.

In 1976 I enlisted in the United States Army. I loved it so much that I stayed in the United States Army and re-enlisted in the Army Reserves and the Army National Guard before joining the United States Army Reserve Command. Once again I re-enlisted in the Army Reserve and the Army National Guard. Finally I joined Active Guard and Reserve in 1982.

I obtained my first civilian job in October 1976. Since then I have worked in many fields of civilian employment and I have obtained many skills I now use. I have worked in the farm field, lawn maintenance, auto mechanic, with the United States Postal Service, Security Guard, Loan Processor, Mover, Deputy Sheriff, Warehouse Manager, and over a dozen other occupations. On my way to obtaining a second Masters degree I was also working with the Department of Homeland Security as a contractor. I possess so many skills that I hope to have the American people looking for some type of employment when they read about all of my skills in the next post.

Today I work for the Los Angeles World Airports as a Driver.

Question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?

Answer: I am a 52 year old man, I was born in Texas and grew up in Arizona. During my summers as a youth we hiked, built forts, and camped. Being physically fit was important to me. This occurred before technology was commonplace, and we couldn’t communicate with each other electronically. I’m a 4th degree black belt in the Korean Martial Art of Taekwondo. What I have learned in this, and other martial arts, to be focused and assertive. I take life seriously and try to be respectful to everyone. My father was a plumber, and there were times when my family was struggling to make ends meet. I worked with my dad during the summers to help make ends meet. One time I stole from my boss at his construction site. I was so embarrassed. He was like a father figure to me, and this was the first time I had ever stole, much less from a family member. Although he and my father forgave me, it was embarrassing and it taught me to never take advantage of family. They love me and have never let me down.

I have a loving wife of 29 years, 3 sons, 6 grand kids. I work as a Driver Trainer for my company. Most of my time is spent on the road, either in my truck or in a car with a student. We work together to ensure our safety and the safety of other drivers on the highway. I eat good healthy foods and believe in preventing myself from getting sick rather than trying to get better once I’m sick. So, I try to sleep regularly, exercise, and eat healthy foods.

When I’m not driving I might be at home with family or I enjoy working on cars and trucks. I love cruising in my 1964 Mustang convertible in the summer, especially on a weekend during the day. As long as it’s not windy I can’t get enough of the convertible, and I enjoy the attention.

I enjoy making dinner from scratch at home, although I don’t do it often. I enjoy learning about historic and prehistoric peoples. My wife and I were married in a tribe in Arizona, and we were blessed by two elders. I’m curious about them, and I love watching the nature channel and learning about animals, not just those we eat. My wife is a vegetarian and doesn’t eat meat. I do eat meat, but I don’t kill the animals. I’m an animal lover, and I care for all animals.

I was in a bad wreck one time in my 64 Mustang convertible, but it was the off season and it wasn’t my fault. There were no injuries, and no one was hurt. I was traveling about 90 mph and it was nighttime. This was a long time ago, but I still remember the gravel road, which threw my car into the ditch. After this incident I decided that I shouldn’t be driving so fast and that driving like a maniac wasn’t worth my life. So, I learned the lesson. The trees and the dirt weren’t the only things flying by my car. I slowed down, but occasionally I will take a fast, twisty road and have some fun with my friends.

In the military I made sure to go home every weekend. My Mom and Dad were always worried and on the phone. I promised them I would come home every weekend.

The past is the past and I am who I am. I love my family and I’m proud of who I am. I don’t make myself happy, I make them happy.
